texto: There are times when I can find myself in a book, too, for two or three hours. But afterward I have such an urge to go out and reach for other people. Very often they're not around. There's also a metaphysical loneliness. We all feel it. The burden of living one's own life is experiencing sensations that no one else can share. You take a step in a house, you start moving around the house, no one else moves with you. You're walking by yourself.
explicacao: Citação de David Ignatow sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “There are times when I can find myself in a book, too, for two or three hours. But afterward I ha…” articula vida, lonliness, solitude em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como solitude.

frase_original: There are times when I can find myself in a book, too, for two or three hours. But afterward I have such an urge to go out and reach for other people. Very often they're not around. There's also a metaphysical loneliness. We all feel it. The burden of living one's own life is experiencing sensations that no one else can share. You take a step in a house, you start moving around the house, no one else moves with you. You're walking by yourself.

texto: Standing beside you,I took an oathto make your life simpler by complicating mineand what I always thought would happen did:I was lifted up in joy.

texto: I should be contentto look at a mountainfor what it isand not as a comment on my life.
explicacao: Orientação direta de David Ignatow sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “I should be contentto look at a mountainfor what it isand not as a comment on my life.” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, contentment, man, útil em momentos de man.
